title: Generating multiple stage-matched C. elegans hybrids and parental strains simultaneously
author: Avery Davis Bell
materials: Re-use 1mL of buffer on several plates
Flash freeze tubes in liquid nitrogen
Freeze by submerging tube in liquid nitrogen

steps.title: In general, follow standard worm culture procedures (Stiernagle 2006)
steps.instruction: In general, follow standard worm culture procedures (Stiernagle 2006). Grow worms at 18°C or cooler if any strains have known or suspected mortal germline phenotypes (Frezal et al. 2018)

steps.title: Grow healthy cultures of each strain...
steps.instruction: Grow healthy cultures of each strain that will be a parental strain. For the reference strain that will be crossed to all wild strains, make sure to have many plates growing (e.g., 15 growing plates).

steps.title: For all strains that will be...
steps.instruction: For all strains that will be crossed to the common reference strain, start and propagate male-heavy cultures (to have enough males to mate)

steps.title: Set up mating plates with 20-30...
steps.instruction: Set up mating plates with 20-30 hermaphrodites and as many males as you can find (as few as 3 and up to 45) from the wild strain

steps.title: Repeat setting up mating plates until...
steps.instruction: Repeat setting up mating plates until you can visibly see a lot of males; then, these male cultures can be propagated as normal (e.g., through chunking)

steps.title: For all strains, keep the original hermaphrodite-heavy culture going
steps.instruction: For all strains, keep the original hermaphrodite-heavy culture going

steps.title: Day 1
steps.instruction: Day 1. Bleach synchronize all parental strains and re-plate embryos.

steps.title: Inspect plates, only use plates that are not starved
steps.instruction: Inspect plates, only use plates that are not starved.

steps.title: Wash 2-3 plates of the wild...
steps.instruction: Wash 2-3 plates of the wild strains and 10-15 plates of the reference strain to a conical 15 mL tube using water.

steps.title: Centrifuge tubes (2000 rpm for 2...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge tubes (2000 rpm for 2 minutes) and remove the supernatant careful of not disturbing the worm pellet.

steps.title: Resuspend pellet with 7 mL of...
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet with 7 mL of water and add 3 mL of a 2:1 Bleach:NaOH (5M) bleaching solution. Gently shake tubes constantly for 5-7 minutes. During this time, inspect tubes using a stereoscope. When carcasses of the worms are largely dissolved (often around 7 minutes), centrifuge tubes as described before.

steps.title: Remove the bleaching solution from the...
steps.instruction: Remove the bleaching solution from the tube and resuspend the pellet in 13 mL of water. Shake tubes vigorously and centrifuge as before.

steps.title: Repeat previous washing step one more time
steps.instruction: Repeat previous washing step one more time. Remove washing solution (water).

steps.title: Resuspend embryos in water and transfer...
steps.instruction: Resuspend embryos in water and transfer to NGM/Agarose plates as necessary.

steps.title: Make sure to have several growing...
steps.instruction: Make sure to have several growing plates per strain to distribute across temperatures.

steps.title: Grow reference-strain worms on growing plates at multiple temperatures (e
steps.instruction: Grow reference-strain worms on growing plates at multiple temperatures (e.g., 18°C, 19°C, 20°C, and room temperature) to ensure worms at the proper stage of development will be available throughout a wide window when generating mating plates. Grow wild worm cultures at a couple of these temperatures.

steps.title: Day 3 (starting 48 hours after bleach syncing)
steps.instruction: Day 3 (starting 48 hours after bleach syncing): set up mating plates.

steps.title: Add 60 L4 hermaprodites from the...
steps.instruction: Add 60 L4 hermaprodites from the reference strain to each of 5 mating plates per wild strain.

steps.title: Screen these plates to ensure no males are on them
steps.instruction: Screen these plates to ensure no males are on them.

steps.title: Add 40 L4 males from the...
steps.instruction: Add 40 L4 males from the wild strain to the 5 mating plates for that strain.

steps.title: Add 80 L4 hermaphrodites from each...
steps.instruction: Add 80 L4 hermaphrodites from each of the wild strains to each of 3 mating plates for each strain. (The selfed offspring of these worms will be the matched ‘parent’ worms for the F1 crosses)

steps.title: Leave worms to mate for 48 hours at 20°C
steps.instruction: Leave worms to mate for 48 hours at 20°C

steps.title: Day 5 after allowing 48 hours of mating
steps.instruction: Day 5 after allowing 48 hours of mating: wash all worms, bacteria, and embryos from one cross (or parental selfing) into a single 15mL conical tube, being careful to collect all embryos.

steps.title: Rinse plates twice with water, then...
steps.instruction: Rinse plates twice with water, then gently swipe tip of glass pipet back and forth across the surface of the plate while expelling water, holding plate at an angle. Goal is to remove all the embryos but not damage them, and not dislodge any agar into the liquid.

steps.title: Repeat several times, reusing the same...
steps.instruction: Repeat several times, reusing the same water (collecting in bottom edge of plate; very gently but methodically swipe the pipet tip back and forth across the surface of the plate in a comprehensive pattern), until lawn is completely removed by eye.

steps.title: Check under scope, re-focus on rinsing remaining areas as needed
steps.instruction: Check under scope, re-focus on rinsing remaining areas as needed.

steps.title: Add water to 14mL (so that...
steps.instruction: Add water to 14mL (so that all tubes have equal volume)

steps.title: Spin 2000rpm x 2 min
steps.instruction: Spin 2000rpm x 2 min

steps.title: Use vacuum to remove most of...
steps.instruction: Use vacuum to remove most of liquid, taking care not to disrupt loose pellet (remove as much bacteria as possible – but don’t risk the pellet!).

steps.title: Resuspend each tube to 3
steps.instruction: Resuspend each tube to 3.5mL with fresh water

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.5 mL bleaching solution (2:1 bleach:NaOH). Start timer.

steps.title: Shake tubes every 30s, examine
steps.instruction: Shake tubes every 30s, examine.

steps.title: When adult carcasses fully dissolve (often...
steps.instruction: When adult carcasses fully dissolve (often around 7 minutes), add water to each tube up to 14mL.

steps.title: Spin 2000 rpm x 2 min
steps.instruction: Spin 2000 rpm x 2 min.

steps.title: Vacuum and long glass pipet aspirate...
steps.instruction: Vacuum and long glass pipet aspirate bleaching solution, getting as close to pellet as is safe.

steps.title: Resuspend to 10mL with water and shake
steps.instruction: Resuspend to 10mL with water and shake

steps.title: Spin 2000 rpm x 2 min
steps.instruction: Spin 2000 rpm x 2 min.

steps.title: Vacuum aspirate only to the 1
steps.instruction: Vacuum aspirate only to the 1.5mL mark (avoid embryo loss).

steps.title: Resuspend with water + 0
steps.instruction: Resuspend with water + 0.25% tween-20 to 15mL

steps.title: Spin 2000 rpm x 2 min,...
steps.instruction: Spin 2000 rpm x 2 min, being careful to position the tubes within the centrifuge so the tube sides with the interior striations are not on the inside where embryos would collect.

steps.title: Vacuum aspirate as close to the pellet as possible
steps.instruction: Vacuum aspirate as close to the pellet as possible.

steps.title: Resuspend with water (to wash out detergent)
steps.instruction: Resuspend with water (to wash out detergent)

steps.title: Spin 2000 rpm x 2 min
steps.instruction: Spin 2000 rpm x 2 min

steps.title: Vacuum aspirate as close to the pellet as possible
steps.instruction: Vacuum aspirate as close to the pellet as possible

steps.title: Resuspend embryo pellet to 10mL with M9 buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend embryo pellet to 10mL with M9 buffer.

steps.title: Leave tubes gently rocking at room temperature
steps.instruction: Leave tubes gently rocking at room temperature. Embryos will develop into L1s and arrest.

steps.title: Day 6/7 – 30ish hours after leaving embryos in buffer
steps.instruction: Day 6/7 – 30ish hours after leaving embryos in buffer. Add tween-20 to 0.25% to each tube

steps.title: Add 25 μl Tween-20 directly to...
steps.instruction: Add 25 μl Tween-20 directly to the synced worms in tubes

steps.title: OR, combine 500 μl Tween-20 to...
steps.instruction: OR, combine 500 μl Tween-20 to 4mL of water and mix well, then add 200uL of this diluted solution to the synced worms in tubes

steps.title: Spin 2000rpm x 2 min
steps.instruction: Spin 2000rpm x 2 min

steps.title: Aspirate using vacuum, leaving under 0
steps.instruction: Aspirate using vacuum, leaving under 0.5mL volume in tube. The pellet of L1 worms is loose, so take care not to disturb it.

steps.title: Quick count worms to determine their density
steps.instruction: Quick count worms to determine their density. For each tube:

steps.title: Add 10μl of mixed worm mixture...
steps.instruction: Add 10μl of mixed worm mixture as a single droplet to an empty 6cM plate

steps.title: Roughly count the number of L1s...
steps.instruction: Roughly count the number of L1s immediately after adding the droplet by eye under the scope. Record this number.

steps.title: Calculate the volume of liquid containing 400 worms
steps.instruction: Calculate the volume of liquid containing 400 worms

steps.title: Add 400 worms to 3 or...
steps.instruction: Add 400 worms to 3 or more un-syncing plates (6cM large-lawn plates) per strain.

steps.title: Split the 400 worms into multiple...
steps.instruction: Split the 400 worms into multiple smaller spots on the lawn if possible to facilitate drying

steps.title: Leave lids off the plates until...
steps.instruction: Leave lids off the plates until droplets absorb, typically a few minutes

steps.title: Day 8
steps.instruction: Day 8. Remove males from at least 3 plates from each strain. F1s will be ~50% males so this is a challenge; parentals only have spontaneous males so just checking them a couple times should suffice. Males start to be visible ~32 hours after un-syncing, however, they’re very young. Males are much more evident 36+ hours after un-syncing.

steps.title: Day 9
steps.instruction: Day 9. Prepare liquid nitrogen for flash-freezing worms after harvest.

steps.title: Identify strains where most hermaphrodites on...
steps.instruction: Identify strains where most hermaphrodites on the plate are gravid and embryos have been laid on the plate, typically 55-60 hours after un-syncing at 20°C. Several strains may become mature young adults like this at once while others lag; all strains should reach this within a few hours on this day.

steps.title: While looking at strains, remove any...
steps.instruction: While looking at strains, remove any straggler males that made it through the initial screen.

steps.title: Group strains that are developmentally similar...
steps.instruction: Group strains that are developmentally similar and do the following harvesting in these similar groups.

steps.title: Rinse all plates from one strain...
steps.instruction: Rinse all plates from one strain with M9 buffer into 15mL tubes

steps.title: Make sure each plate gets rinsed at least twice
steps.instruction: Make sure each plate gets rinsed at least twice

steps.title: Check to make sure all worms...
steps.instruction: Check to make sure all worms are removed, especially for strains with fewer worms (F1s). Do not scrape up the bacteria or rinse embryos, focus on adult worms.

steps.title: Spin 2000rpm x 2 min
steps.instruction: Spin 2000rpm x 2 min

steps.title: Remove buffer using vacuum down close to pellet
steps.instruction: Remove buffer using vacuum down close to pellet

steps.title: Resuspend in 13 mL M9 buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend in 13 mL M9 buffer.

steps.title: Spin 2000rpm x 2 min
steps.instruction: Spin 2000rpm x 2 min

steps.title: Remove buffer using vacuum down close to pellet
steps.instruction: Remove buffer using vacuum down close to pellet

steps.title: Add TRIzol to a total volume of ~1500 μl /1
steps.instruction: Add TRIzol to a total volume of ~1500 μl /1.5mL. (this is probably adding ~1200uL TRIzol, but try to have final volume of worms + trizol consistent across strains).

steps.title: Transfer equivalent volume of worms in...
steps.instruction: Transfer equivalent volume of worms in trizol to each of 3 1.5mL centrifuge tubes, ending with ~500μl per tube. Using smallest glass pipette works well.

steps.title: Store at -80°C until ready to proceed with RNA extraction
steps.instruction: Store at -80°C until ready to proceed with RNA extraction.

steps.title: Set up for RNA extraction
steps.instruction: Set up for RNA extraction. Full details on reagents and equipment are found in the appropriate sections; this step describes set up for this specific experiment. Plan to extract RNA in batches of no more than 18 samples/batch, with one sample per strain in each batch (likely 1 or 3 batches) Equipment: Heat block set to 37°C Procure dewer of liquid nitrogen for freeze-thawing Centrifuge at (or set to) room temperature Centrifuge at (or set to) 4°C Reagents: TRIzol (fume hood!) Chloroform (fume hood!) Freshly prepared 70% ethanol Qiagen RNeasy buffer RW1 Qiagen RNeasy buffer RPE (with ethanol added as directed by the kit) RNAse-free water Sets of tubes to label: 1.5mL tubes for aqueous phase after TRIzol-chloroform spin RNeasy spin columns 1.5mL tubes for eluted RNA collection Clean with RNAse away: Gloves Fume hood Outside of tip boxes, reagents to be used Freeze thaw area Centrifuge area

steps.title: Vortex worms (previously stored at -80°C...
steps.instruction: Vortex worms (previously stored at -80°C in TRIzol) for 2 minutes

steps.title: Repeat the previous 3 steps for...
steps.instruction: Repeat the previous 3 steps for a total of 6 freeze thaw cycles. Spin down.

steps.title: Add 200μl fresh TRIzol to each tube
steps.instruction: Add 200μl fresh TRIzol to each tube

steps.title: Incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature

steps.title: Add 140μl chloroform to each tube
steps.instruction: Add 140μl chloroform to each tube

steps.title: Shake at 1450 rpm for 1...
steps.instruction: Shake at 1450 rpm for 1 minute (make sure thorough mixing has occurred)

steps.title: Incubate for 2 min at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 2 min at room temperature

steps.title: Spin at 12,000g for 15 minutes at 4°C
steps.instruction: Spin at 12,000g for 15 minutes at 4°C

steps.title: Remove top aqueous phase to new...
steps.instruction: Remove top aqueous phase to new tubes; this is what will be used downstream

steps.title: Add 1 volume of fresh 70%...
steps.instruction: Add 1 volume of fresh 70% ethanol to the aqueous phase; mix and spin down

steps.title: Transfer 650μl of each sample to...
steps.instruction: Transfer 650μl of each sample to a Qiagen RNeasy spin column (1 per sample)

steps.title: Spin at max speed for 30 seconds; discard flow through
steps.instruction: Spin at max speed for 30 seconds; discard flow through

steps.title: Add remaining sample to Qiagen RNEasy...
steps.instruction: Add remaining sample to Qiagen RNEasy spin column with 700μl buffer RW1 (from the Qiagen kit)

steps.title: Spin at max speed for 30 seconds; discard flow through
steps.instruction: Spin at max speed for 30 seconds; discard flow through

steps.title: Add 500 uL buffer RPE (from Qiagen kit)
steps.instruction: Add 500 uL buffer RPE (from Qiagen kit)

steps.title: Spin at max speed for 30 seconds; discard flow through
steps.instruction: Spin at max speed for 30 seconds; discard flow through

steps.title: Repeat buffer RPE wash (add, spin,...
steps.instruction: Repeat buffer RPE wash (add, spin, discard – previous two steps)

steps.title: Spin column at max speed for...
steps.instruction: Spin column at max speed for 2 minutes to remove any residual buffer

steps.title: Transfer spin column to new tube...
steps.instruction: Transfer spin column to new tube (labeled, where RNA will be collected)

steps.title: Add 50uL RNAse-free water to column
steps.instruction: Add 50uL RNAse-free water to column

steps.title: Incubate for 1 min at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 1 min at room temperature

steps.title: Spin at max speed for one minute, retain eluant
steps.instruction: Spin at max speed for one minute, retain eluant

steps.title: Place samples on ice; quantify; store at -80°C
steps.instruction: Place samples on ice; quantify; store at -80°C
